name: Avoid Disinformation
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 2: Meaningful Names
problem: Names carry false or misleading connotations that contradict the actual type, structure, or purpose of the identifier, causing readers to draw incorrect conclusions about the code.
steps: Never use a name that implies a well-known data structure unless the identifier actually uses it (e.g., do not name a HashSet accountList).
Avoid platform or library keywords as variable names (e.g., hp, aix, sco).
Do not use names that are visually similar and differ only in subtle ways.
Never use lowercase l or uppercase O as variable names because they resemble 1 and 0.
Review names in context to verify they accurately describe what the identifier holds or does.
